blockingIssues.description: `result_decomps` is not restored on block exit, so shadowing a `Result` variable inside a block can leak the inner decomposition and corrupt later outer-variable `match` lowering.

Top-level `Result` lets and matches still assume Go multi-return pairs, so direct value-position deeply nested `Result` constructor expressions typecheck but lower to invalid Go instead of the new `Result[T, E]` struct form.

nonBlockingIssues.description: The added regression tests only cover straight-line `Result` variable matches and miss scope-exit/shadowing cases that would catch the `result_decomps` leak.
The new deep nested `Result` runtime regression only exercises the function-return path and does not cover direct top-level ascribed `Result` matches or let-bindings.
